title: hieracium murorum subsp. adcopholepium
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. adcopholepium, a lesser-known species of Hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. This plant is said to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which can be used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Hieracium murorum subsp. adcopholepium include its potential to reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be used topically to soothe skin irritations and promote healing.
